title: All-the-Veg Fried Rice
summary: A quick and healthy dinner for all the family. This fried rice is packed with veg, great for kids and even better with a crispy fried egg and spoonful of crispy chilli oil on top for grown-ups.

ingredients.label: ½ Pointed Cabbage or ¼ White Cabbage
ingredients.name: White Cabbage

ingredients.label: ½ Pointed Cabbage or ¼ White Cabbage
ingredients.name: Cabbage

ingredients.label: 250g Cooked Rice or 1 x 250g Microwavable Rice Pouch
ingredients.name: Microwave Rice

ingredients.label: 250g Cooked Rice or 1 x 250g Microwavable Rice Pouch
ingredients.name: Rice

method.description:
1 Adult Serving = 2 Kids Servings 
Finely chop the onion, carrot, courgette, red pepper and cabbage.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large frying pan over a medium-high heat. Add all the chopped vegetables and stir-fry for 6-7 mins, tossing regularly, until completely soft.
Peel and finely chop the ginger and garlic, then add them to the pan. Fry for 1 min until fragrant.
Add the cooked rice and frozen peas. Stir-fry for 1 min to heat through, then push everything to one side of the pan.
Crack the eggs into the empty space and leave to set for 30 seconds. Use a spoon or spatula to gently scramble the eggs, then fold them through the rice and veg.
Splash in the soy sauce, stir well and season to taste.

If making the crispy chilli egg, set a small saucepan on a medium heat. 
Add a drizzle of chilli oil and crack in the egg. Fry until cooked to your desired done-ness, we like a runny yolk!

Serve up! 
Kids Plate: Dish up the rice as is. 
Parents Plate: Top adult portions with a crispy fried egg, chopped coriander and a spoonful of extra crispy chilli oil if you want more heat.

tips: Great for using up leftover rice — just make sure it’s fully chilled first.
Add tofu, chicken or prawns for extra protein.
Works well with whatever veg you’ve got kicking about in the fridge.

chefs.name: Adam Bush
chefs.summary: Adam is a chef, baker and food writer with a love of big, bold flavours. He trained in Michelin-starred kitchens, pubs and a bakery before turning his hand to recipe writing.
